title: Scourge Survivors
tagline: Drop into the breach. Get swarmed. Burn your way deeper until it's over.
overview: You are a lone Pyre operator dropped into a breach with one job: survive the Scourge. The horde never stops growing, so neither can you. Auto-scaling weapons, XP, and level-up drafts meet DOOM-fast gunfeel and gore.

features.title: Survive, then go deeper
features.desc: Drop in, get swarmed, grind the Scourge, level up, draft upgrades, and push room to room until you're overrun.

features.title: The loop IS the game
features.desc: No side-mode filler. The Vampire-Survivors core — orbit, bolt, nova — wears DOOM's skin and never lets up.

features.title: Breach-arena maps
features.desc: Every chamber of the breach is its own arena of rooms and levels, with the deepest reaches of Perdition waiting below.

title: Deadlane
tagline: Hold the lane. The breach is open and the Scourge is already coming.
overview: You are the Wardens, and the line is yours to hold. The Scourge floods every chokepoint in numbers no charge could ever clear — so you build, you wall, and you bleed the horde dry. Every lane held is a generation bought.

features.title: Hold the chokepoint
features.desc: Each level is a named lane. Lay down Warden tech, break the swarm's density, and make the breach pay for every step.

features.title: Draft and synergize
features.desc: Wave pressure earns scrap, scrap funds 1-of-3 tower and commander drafts, and the right builds chain into something brutal.

features.title: One war, two fronts
features.desc: Shares the horde and the look of Scourge Survivors — where they go in, you keep the wall standing behind them.

title: Pactfall
tagline: Settle the grudge in the arena. Just don't let the Pact break.
overview: The current playable prototype tests the first arena slice: Pyre pressure, a Warden opponent, lane minions, and a neutral Scourge problem in the center. The design target is still the full human rivalry made playable — Pyre and Wardens settling doctrine grudges in sanctioned blood-arenas, fought over lanes, never as real war.

features.title: Pyre vs Wardens
features.desc: Current build proves the arena pressure first; the target is hero play for both creeds once the loop earns the extra roster work.

features.title: The Scourge twist
features.desc: Ignore the neutral horde in the center and it overruns both teams. A big enough surge can force a momentary truce — because humans always unite against it.

features.title: Built on the run loop
features.desc: Escalating lane pressure, XP and blood drops, 1-of-3 drafts, build synergies, and boss-objective gates pulled straight from canon.

title: Brawl
tagline: Pick a fighter. Let the front choose the rival. Settle the clash before the Scourge takes the field.
overview:
Brawl turns the Warline front into character-scale violence: Pyre, Wardens, and Scourge champions dragged into one-on-one battlefield fights where a single duel can rally a contested lane.

The first playable version is a production-learning slice for fighting-game sprites and AI-assisted game building. It proves character select, rival assignment, readable hit timing, block pressure, KO, and rematch before the real fighting sprite sheets land.

features.title: Rival chosen by the front
features.desc: Pick your fighter, then the game assigns the opposing champion so the duel feels like a Warline incident, not a menu mirror match.

features.title: Fighting sprite lab
features.desc: The target asset pipeline covers idle, walk, jump, guard, light, heavy, special, hurt, knockdown, KO, and win reads.

features.title: Battlefield results
features.desc: Wins and losses can bank into the shared war record and later report into Warline as contested-front pressure.

title: Starblight
tagline: Burn the infection out of the sky. Remember the night it fell.
overview: Starblight is Scourge contamination loose in orbit — spores, infected wreckage, living carrier-ships, and breach matter spreading through the void. Zero Day is folded into this game as the first-contact last stand: the doomed night the sky fell. The current playable prototype foregrounds a lone Warden interceptor; the design target expands that into Pyre and Warden pilot identities intercepting the rot before it touches the ground war below.

features.title: Zero Day prologue
features.desc: The first-contact last stand lives here now: pre-schism pilots, evacuation lanes, and a battle that canonically ends in loss.

features.title: Pilot buildcraft
features.desc: Top-down mouse-flight survivors on the shared run loop: launch, survive escalating swarms, stack ship systems, and burn the orbital boss.

features.title: Parasite conquest in space
features.desc: Toxic-green breach organs, chitin over rusted hulls, living spore mines, and carrier-bosses grown around breach hearts.

title: Redline
tagline: Outrun the Choir. Stop moving and the swarm catches up.
overview: When the Scourge cuts the lanes and chokes the comms between holdouts, the message still has to get through — so you carry it on foot, faster than the horde can close. The current playable prototype follows a Pyre courier first; the full target adds the Warden courier identity once the speed loop is strong enough to carry both creeds.

features.title: Speed beats omniscience
features.desc: The massed Scourge is near-omniscient, but its coordination has a tempo. Hold full redline and you outpace the swarm before it can cut you off.

features.title: Lanes as racecourses
features.desc: Chain ramps, rail-grinds, and breach-debris boost pads through collapsing chokepoints to reach the next holdout before the gate closes.

features.title: The road turns on you
features.desc: Chitin over the asphalt, spore-walls blooming across the lane, Render-class horrors at the junctions, and repeater-pylons that drag your redline down.

title: Rothulk
tagline: Burn it from the inside. Climb the spine of the nest and light the core.
overview: A rothulk is a beached Scourge breach-ship grown into the ground — a mountain of rotting host-flesh and dead-world machinery with a heartbeat. The current playable prototype tests the lone Pyre saboteur climb first: platform up its spine, reach the breach-core, and set it on fire.

features.title: The level is the enemy
features.desc: Ascend chambers of pulsing host-flesh, ride spore-geysers, and crush living platforms on the way to the heart that keeps the nest connected.

features.title: Stay out of the light
features.desc: While the core is lit, the whole hulk knows where you are. Route around the Choir's massed clusters — the gaps between them are your path.

features.title: Ignite and run
features.desc: Reach the breach-core, burn it, then platform back out ahead of the collapse as the severed node goes feral around you.

title: Warline
tagline: The war console. Every breach purged, lane held, and run delivered feeds the same front.
overview: Warline is the strategic layer that makes the other games matter to the same war. It tracks where the Pact is holding, where the Scourge is pressuring the lanes, and which operations buy the world more time — spend shared resources, fortify regions, muster the Pact, recon dark sectors, and push back the Scourge.

features.title: One Living Front
features.desc: A shared persistent map of regions, lanes, and breaches that every player sees and shapes.

features.title: Operation Contract
features.desc: Each game reports an operation: purge a breach, hold the lane, contest territory, intercept orbit, run logistics, sabotage a breach.

features.title: Four Resources
features.desc: Scrap, fuel, biomass, and intel — earned by play, spent on Fortify, Muster, Deploy, and Recon commands.

features.title: Scourge Pressure
features.desc: The Choir pushes back. Ignore a front and the pressure meters climb until a lane falls.
